rationale: In the visual aesthetic feature development section, the citing paper uses Gorn et al. (2004) to justify that color in digital media affects viewers' arousal and perceptions, and specifically that warm hues can increase excitement. The citation is not just in a generic literature list; it is tied to the paper's feature construction choices (warm hue proportion, saturation, brightness) and to expected behavioral relevance. The authors explicitly position their work as extending prior color research to online instructional videos, indicating a meaningful conceptual carryover from the cited study.
relation_description: The citing paper applies insights from Gorn et al. (2004) on screen color effects in web contexts to a new domain, using them to motivate color-based video features for predicting online course consumption behavior.
